scenarios.detection: Post-deploy customer, health, readiness, Access, and error-rate checks identify regression while D1/R2 integrity remains unchanged.
scenarios.containment: Stop rollout and all unrelated releases; do not change storage or use Pages.
scenarios.recovery_path: With explicit rollback approval, target exact fully bound Worker version 92516f62-b891-4903-94e1-204a972ee2ae and record the resulting deployment.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Jeff approves the exact Worker/version and incident commander confirms this is code-only.
scenarios.verification: Verify version traffic, public journeys, Access routes, health/readiness, schedules, bindings by name/type, and logs.
scenarios.residual_gap: No Worker rollback was executed; an approved operational rehearsal remains required.

scenarios.detection: Integrity, reconciliation, audit events, or customer reports identify a bounded bad-write window.
scenarios.containment: Stop every D1 writer, preserve a fresh export and current bookmark, and prevent imports/schedules from replaying the fault.
scenarios.recovery_path: Choose either D1 Time Travel from an incident-supported bookmark or a fresh target restored from a verified SQL export; never paste a dump over live tables.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Jeff separately approves database ID, recovery point, method, writer shutdown, and any binding cutover.
scenarios.verification: Integrity, foreign keys, schema, critical counts, sampled reads, application reads, and writer re-enable checklist.
scenarios.residual_gap: Only two separate-day exports exist; offsite copy and remote non-production restoration remain unproved.

scenarios.detection: Migration or application smoke checks show missing/incompatible schema or partial behavior.
scenarios.containment: Keep affected feature flags off, stop writers that depend on the schema, and preserve pre-change evidence.
scenarios.recovery_path: Prefer an approved forward migration tested on a representative restore; use Time Travel or fresh-target recovery only when incident evidence supports full data rollback.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Migration, database restore, binding change, and application rollback are distinct approvals.
scenarios.verification: Migration ledger, constraints, counts, old/new application reads, and reconciliation against the release packet.
scenarios.residual_gap: Candidate-specific migration rehearsal and remote non-production proof are absent.

scenarios.detection: Missing object responses, D1-to-R2 inventory mismatch, upload audit, or customer report identifies loss/corruption.
scenarios.containment: Disable photo writes, preserve bucket inventory and affected D1 keys, and avoid clearing references until evidence is captured.
scenarios.recovery_path: Restore only from an independently hashed encrypted copy into a separate non-production prefix/bucket, then use an approved promotion plan.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Bucket creation, credentials, copy/export, restore, deletion, and D1 reference changes each require explicit approval.
scenarios.verification: Object count, key/hash/content-type/size comparison, representative image reads, D1 reference reconciliation, and access controls.
scenarios.residual_gap: No independent R2 inventory/export or representative restore exists; this remains a stop-ship for autonomous deletion and customer media.

scenarios.detection: Binding/runtime checks show KV loss or unavailability.
scenarios.containment: Do not treat KV as authoritative data and keep public browsing available.
scenarios.recovery_path: Current code does not read or write SESSION; verify that remains true, then restore/rebind only under an approved future feature-specific plan.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Any namespace creation, rebind, copy, or customer-session behavior requires explicit configuration approval.
scenarios.verification: Repository search, deployment binding check, public journeys, and absence of customer identity/session dependency.
scenarios.residual_gap: Live key contents were not inspected; future SESSION use must introduce its own retention and recovery contract.

scenarios.detection: An operator proposes routing traffic to the retained Pages project during an incident.
scenarios.containment: Reject Pages as a rollback target and keep its hostname out of customer recovery instructions.
scenarios.recovery_path: Use the pinned fully bound Worker version or a separately built and protected Worker recovery deployment.
scenarios.approval_boundary: Any Pages retirement/protection, DNS/routing, or recovery deployment requires explicit approval.
scenarios.verification: Anonymous admin-path probe, public journey checks, Access coverage, and exact Worker version receipt.
scenarios.residual_gap: The Pages hostname still exposes historical administrative artifacts and requires owner-approved containment.
